channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring
item.description: The number of cases — and deaths — in Bangladesh is staggering. As of Sunday, 528 have died, mostly children. How did this measles outbreak begin? And how is the country responding?
item.content:encoded:
The number of cases — and deaths — in Bangladesh is staggering. As of Sunday, 528 have died, mostly children. How did this measles outbreak begin? And how is the country responding?

(Image credit: Anike Rahman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Mind-bending photos by anonymous cousins show the pain and dreams of Afghan women
item.description: The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.
item.content:encoded:
The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.

(Image credit: Mahnaz Ebrahimi|January 2026)
item.dc:creator: Diane Cole

item.title: Religious leaders, lawmakers push for $1 billion to secure houses of worship
item.description: There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.
item.content:encoded:
There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.

(Image credit: Photo courtesy of the Jewish Federations of North America)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Nurse convicted in patient's death is now a national speaker on hospital safety
item.description: RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: Mark Humphrey)
item.dc:creator: Blake Farmer

item.title: Russia pounds Kyiv in powerful drone and missile attack
item.description: At least two people were killed and 77 injured in the attack, which included the use of a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile called the Oreshnik, which is capable of carrying a nuclear warhead.
item.content:encoded:
At least two people were killed and 77 injured in the attack, which included the use of a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile called the Oreshnik, which is capable of carrying a nuclear warhead.

(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: 'Homeland Security' has spawned political insecurity since DHS was born
item.description: DHS was originally conceived in the interest of unity and harmony — and the phrase "homeland security" was originally meant to be reassuring.
item.content:encoded:
DHS was originally conceived in the interest of unity and harmony — and the phrase "homeland security" was originally meant to be reassuring.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Ron Elving

item.title: The Enhanced Games are Sunday. Here's what to know about the controversial event
item.description: Dozens of athletes — including former Olympians — will participate in the Las Vegas event while using performance-enhancing drugs.
item.content:encoded:
Dozens of athletes — including former Olympians — will participate in the Las Vegas event while using performance-enhancing drugs.

(Image credit: Ty ONeil)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Torn by war, Israelis and Palestinians tie their fortunes together
item.description: At a time when hopes are dim for resolving the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, some Arab and Jewish entrepreneurs are partnering across the divide, hoping to prove what's possible.
item.content:encoded:
At a time when hopes are dim for resolving the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, some Arab and Jewish entrepreneurs are partnering across the divide, hoping to prove what's possible.

(Image credit: Dena Yadin)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Suspect dead after opening fire near White House security checkpoint, Secret Service says
item.description: A man who opened fire Saturday near a White House security checkpoint is dead after being shot by officers who returned fire, the U.S. Secret Service said. It was the third incidence of gunfire in the vicinity of President Donald Trump in the past month.
item.content:encoded:
A man who opened fire Saturday near a White House security checkpoint is dead after being shot by officers who returned fire, the U.S. Secret Service said. It was the third incidence of gunfire in the vicinity of President Donald Trump in the past month.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
item.description: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.
item.content:encoded:
Fjord — and Sebastian Stan, during the awards ceremony at the 79th Cannes international film festival, in southern France, on Saturday.'/>

Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

(Image credit: Andreea Alexandru)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster
